description_long: This is a vintage-style kerosene lantern, predominantly constructed from metal and finished in a matte reddish-brown color. The lantern features a rounded base with a fuel cap, a central cylindrical chamber where the light source would be housed, and a protective wire cage that encircles the body to shield the glass and internal components. A wide, flat metal top acts as a heat shield, and a curved handle is attached at the top for carrying or hanging. The lantern lacks visible glass panels, suggesting either a missing or implied glass enclosure. Its utilitarian design is typical of lanterns used in outdoor or urban settings for portable lighting.
description: A vintage metal kerosene lantern with a wire cage, rounded base, and a curved handle, finished in matte reddish-brown. The design is utilitarian and lacks visible glass panels.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wire cage, rounded base, and central chamber with the handle arching above.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the side profile of the wire cage and the offset fuel cap on the base.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the back of the wire cage and the solid, rounded base.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the curvature of the handle and the symmetry of the wire cage.
